title: requests TLS bypass
subtitle: Session verify=False stickiness
query: Assess CVE-2024-35195 in the Python requests library and how urgently to patch.

data.query: Assess CVE-2024-35195 in the Python requests library and how urgently to patch.

data.summary: CVE-2024-35195 is a MEDIUM-severity (CVSS 5.6) logic flaw in the Python `requests` library (< 2.32.0). When a Session makes its first request with `verify=False`, TLS certificate verification is silently disabled for all subsequent requests to the same host — even if `verify` is later reset to `True` — enabling potential man-in-the-middle attacks. Fixed in requests 2.32.0.
data.recommended_action: SSVC: Track* — Upgrade requests to 2.32.0+ (`pip install --upgrade requests`). No public exploits, not in CISA KEV, EPSS is 0.34% (26th percentile). Patch within standard cycle; also audit Session code for any `verify=False` usage that could trigger this sticky-disable behavior.

cves.summary: In Python `requests` < 2.32.0, setting `verify=False` on the first Session request permanently disables TLS certificate verification for that host across the connection pool lifetime, silently exposing apps to MitM attacks even after `verify=True` is set.

reasoning_chain: cve_lookup(CVE-2024-35195) -> CVSS 5.6 MEDIUM, CWE-670 (Always-Incorrect Control Flow), affects requests < 2.32.0; verify=False stickiness bug in Session connection pool
exploit_check(CVE-2024-35195) -> no public exploits on Exploit-DB or GitHub
kev_check(CVE-2024-35195) -> not in CISA KEV catalog
epss_score(CVE-2024-35195) -> probability 0.34%, 26th percentile; low active exploitation likelihood
patch_lookup(CVE-2024-35195) -> NVD has no CPE-bound fixed_entries; GitHub advisory and NVD description confirm fix in requests 2.32.0
attack_mapping([CWE-670]) -> no ATT&CK techniques mapped for CWE-670
Decision: SSVC Track* — no active exploitation signals; patch within standard cycle

ssvc.rationale: SSVC decision Track: CVE-2024-35195 no active-exploitation, public-exploit, or high-EPSS signal observed.
